label: Battery Controller v2
info:
Cost-optimized battery charge/discharge controller.
Queries InfluxDB for prices, solar forecast, SOC, and load.
Outputs optimal state for each 15-min price interval.

name: AC Load
query: SELECT LAST(acload) AS acload FROM ess WHERE time > now() - 30m

name: Power
query: SELECT LAST(Power) AS power FROM ess WHERE time > now() - 30m

name: Cache & topic: prices
Solar
query: SELECT sunshineDurationInMinutes FROM forecastnew WHERE time >= now() - 1h ORDER BY time ASC

name: Load History
query: SELECT mean(acload) AS avg_load FROM ess WHERE time > now() - 7d GROUP BY time(1h) ORDER BY time ASC

name: Add Weather Context
Battery Optimizer
=== Battery Controller v2 - Main Flow ===
info:
Runs every 15 minutes.
1. Queries InfluxDB for SOC, prices, solar forecast, load
2. Optimizes battery state for each price interval
3. Writes plan to batterycontrol.plan
4. Outputs current setpoint

name: === Test Cases ===
info: Three test scenarios using historic/simulated data

name: Test 1: Sunny Apr 7 neg prices
Need fresh prices?
Retry if no tomorrow
Read cached prices
PV Now
query: SELECT LAST(PAC) AS pv_now FROM pv WHERE time > now() - 30m

name: PV Hist recent
query: SELECT mean(mean_PAC) AS avg_pv, max(mean_PAC) AS max_pv FROM pv WHERE time > now() - 15d GROUP BY time(1h) ORDER BY time ASC

name: PV Hist 1y ago
query: SELECT mean(mean_PAC) AS avg_pv, max(mean_PAC) AS max_pv FROM pv WHERE time > now() - 380d AND time < now() - 350d GROUP BY time(1h) ORDER BY time ASC

name: PV Hist 2y ago
query: SELECT mean(mean_PAC) AS avg_pv, max(mean_PAC) AS max_pv FROM pv WHERE time > now() - 745d AND time < now() - 715d GROUP BY time(1h) ORDER BY time ASC

name: Merge & topic: pv_history
=== Changelog ===
info:
### 2026-05-10 (Phase 3d: pvOnlyOverflow gates pvWillCurtail + isOverflowOnly)
- User report: tomorrow's plan feeds in at 15.75/16ct in the morning
  (05:30 & 06:00 UTC) then grid-charges 16 slots midday at mp 10–12ct.
  Round-trip = sell 15.75ct, refill at 22ct effective (mp + 10.4ct
  discount fee) → ~6ct/kWh loss. Weak-PV day (peak 657W); no actual
  PV curtailment possible.
- Root cause: Phase 3d's `pvWillCurtail` flag AND its `isOverflowOnly`
  shortcut both read from `overflowSoc`/`postHorizonOverflow`, which
  are computed by a projection that adds `_plan='charge'` slots. On
  weak-PV days Phase 3c plans aggressive midday grid charging that
  pushes projSoc past 99, so the optimizer thinks PV will curtail and
  treats the first feed-in pick as "free overflow" — bypassing the
  round-trip check.
- Fix: compute a separate `pvOnlyOverflow` from a PV–load-only walk
  (no charge/feed-in plans). Use it for BOTH `pvWillCurtail` and the
  `isOverflowOnly` shortcut in the candidate loop. The first pick now
  has to satisfy `mp > replacementPrice` like every other pick.

### 2026-05-09 (Reserve cap raised 12→21 kWh)
- User: "wenn man den strom vom grid kaufen muss ist es ganz schlecht"
  — feed-in tonight at 16ct losing money if forced to grid-charge Mon
  morning at retail (~30ct). The 12 kWh cap (40% SOC) was the binding
  constraint: walk computed ~34 kWh deficit for the dead-PV streak Mon-Tue
  but got clipped at 12. End-SOC tomorrow only rose to 50% — still below
  the daily-load envelope (~17 kWh ≈ 57% SOC).
- Fix: MAX_RESERVE_KWH 12 → 21 kWh (~70% SOC reserve). End-of-horizon
  target now climbs to ~80% on dead-PV forecasts, which blocks almost all
  feed-in tonight (SOC 86% → ~80% = ~2 kWh budget). Cap is non-binding
  on good-weather forecasts because the walk already weighs PV via
  getSunshineForecast — sunshine eats most of the deficit on sunny days.

### 2026-05-09 (Multi-day post-horizon reserve)
- User report: optimizer planned 13 kWh feed-in tonight (mp 14-17ct)
  draining SOC 86% → 31%, with end-of-tomorrow target 30%. But weather
  forecast shows Mon-Sat all <2h sunshine each (heavy rain). Tomorrow's
  modest PV refill (~20 kWh) won't last the streak; would force grid-
  charge at peak Mon morning. Root cause: post-schedule reserve walk only
  covered 14h and capped at 6 kWh (~20% SOC). The slightly-negative
  tomorrow prices (-0.24ct min) tripped the "strong solar" rule and set
  end-of-horizon target to 30%, with no signal that the day after was a
  PV desert.
- Fix: extended reserve walk RESERVE_HOURS 14 → 48 and raised cap
  MAX_RESERVE_KWH 6 → 12 (40% SOC on a 30 kWh battery). The walk already
  weighs PV via getSunshineForecast (forecastnew has 7d hourly data), so
  good-weather days don't pad the reserve — only sustained PV holes do.
  When the next 2 days look bad, end-slot target rises (minTargetSoc +
  reserve%), which feeds back to feed-in budget (line 936) and runtime
  socNeeded (line 1330), blocking aggressive overnight feed-in.

### 2026-05-09 (Phase 3a: DP-based preemptive pick)
- User report: 06:30/06:45 fed in at mp 13.51/12.85 ct while 08:00
  (mp 13.98 ct, the day's HIGHEST) only held (state=3). Root cause:
  preemptive sorted candidates by mp DESC and committed top-N without
  modeling chronological SOC depletion. Phase 4's runtime gate
  `soc > target+3` then demoted the high-mp later slot because earlier
  picks had drained SOC below the gate at its time. Net: the best slot
  of the day silently lost to a lower-mp earlier sibling.
- Fix: replaced sort-by-mp DESC pick with a 2D DP over (slot_idx,
  soc_bucket). Maximizes Σ(mp × reliefSoc) subject to (a) round-trip
  filter mp > minReplacementEffPrice and (b) chronological gate
  pre-soc > target+3. SOC quantized to 0.5%; ~50 slots × 200 buckets
  trivial. Removed extraDrainNeeded cap: every DP pick is round-trip
  profitable, so over-drain is net-revenue-positive (and the gate
  bounds total drain anyway). reliefSoc uses the same physical metric
  as Phase 3d (state=3 vs state=4 SOC delta), so high-PV slots with
  drainW=0 still earn their (pv-load) credit.
- Regression 9/9 pass.

### 2026-05-07b (Phase 3d: budget uses net relief, cap at 99, round-up)
- After 5/7a fix, evening peaks were picked but tomorrow midday still
  forced 1-2 curtailment slots at 6-8 ct. Three compounding errors:
  (1) Budget loop compared full feedinDrainSoc (3.5%/slot) vs projected
      overflow, but state=3 would have drained ~0.58%/slot anyway —
      effective relief per feed-in slot is only ~2.92%. Picked too few.
  (2) Projection capped overflow at 100% but the runtime curtailment
      branch fires at soc≥99 — 1% mismatch left a stray curtail slot.
  (3) Discretization: budget 19.3% / 2.92% per slot rounds DOWN to 6
      slots (17.52% relief), leaving 1.78% residual to curtail.
- Fix: (1) new feedinReliefSoc(slot) = feedinDrainSoc + (pv-load)*soc;
       (2) projection caps at 99 to align with runtime trigger;
       (3) loop break flips from "would-overshoot" to "already-met" —
           pick the slot that crosses budget if mp still beats
           replacementPrice. The overshoot is bounded by 1 slot.
- Verified live: 7 evening slots picked (mp 18.89-21.88 ct);
  tomorrow midday peaks at 97.7% SOC, ZERO curtailment slots.
  Regression 9/9 pass.

### 2026-05-07 (Phase 3d: pvWillCurtail relax PV-opportunity gate)
- Round-trip guard still blocked evening feed-in when tomorrow's
  curtailment mp was positive-but-low. Today (5/7): 17-22 ct evening
  peak skipped because cheapPvRefillAhead (mp<3) was false — tomorrow's
  midday lowest mp is 5.45 ct. replacementPrice fell back to
  minReplacementEffPrice (~24.75 ct from pre-sunrise grid recharge)
  and 21.88ct evening peak failed 21.88 > 24.75. Same SOC then dumped
  via forced curtailment at 6-7 ct.
- Fix: gate pvOpportunityPrice on (cheapPvRefillAhead || pvWillCurtail)
  where pvWillCurtail = (overflowSoc + postHorizonOverflow) > 0. When
  PV will saturate the battery anyway, the SOC drained tonight is
  displaced from a forced curtailment slot, not a hypothetical grid
  recharge — correct replacement cost is the forgone curtailment
  revenue (~7-9 ct), not pre-sunrise grid (~24 ct).
- Verified live: 4 evening slots picked at 21.88/20.32/20.33/20.13 ct;
  tomorrow's curtailment shrunk from 5 slots to 2. Regression 9/9 pass.

### 2026-05-06 (Saturation cluster + round-trip-aware runtime)
- Phase 4 runtime "battery full + PV surplus" branch was firing
  opportunistically at the FIRST slot crossing soc≥95 with mp>floor,
  ignoring round-trip economics. Today (5/6) at 16:00 it fed-in at
  mp=9.06ct (local minimum of the day) while replacement cost was
  ~22ct/kWh — a -13ct/kWh loss. The same saturation cluster contained
  17:30-17:45 at mp=13-14ct, also a loser vs replacement (weak PV
  forecast tomorrow), so the right answer was: don't feed in at all.
- Fix: hoisted `replacementPrice` out of the Phase 3d block so the
  runtime branch can use the same round-trip threshold.
- Phase 4 "battery full + PV surplus" branch now fires only when
  (a) soc≥99 (imminent curtailment, sell at any mp>FEEDIN_MIN_MP_CT)
  or (b) mp>replacementPrice (round-trip profitable). Otherwise
  state=3 (hold), preserving SOC for tomorrow's load.
- New Phase 3d.2 "Saturation cluster" pass: walks the passive
  projection (`_plannedSoc`), finds consecutive (soc≥95 + PV>load+500)
  runs, and marks the highest-mp slot per cluster as
  `feedin_saturation` IFF mp>replacementPrice. Without this, on
  profitable cluster days the runtime would still fire at the first
  cluster slot rather than the best-mp one.
- Planned-feedin branch (Phase 4) now also handles `feedin_saturation`.

### 2026-05-05 (FEEDIN_MIN_MP_CT floor)
- New constant FEEDIN_MIN_MP_CT=1 prevents feed-in (planned or runtime) at
  sub-1ct marketPrice. Today produced state=4 at mp=0.14ct (12:30 UTC,
  soc=96%, PV=4640W) via the Phase 4 "Battery full + PV surplus, mp > 0"
  branch — sub-cent revenue does not cover cycle wear / inverter losses.
  Floor applied at Phase 3d candidate filter (was mp > 0), Phase 3e
  minThreshold (was Math.max(0.01, ...)), and Phase 4 runtime branch
  (was mp > 0). Phase 3d overflow-exempt slots also affected — even
  truly curtailed PV is not worth cycling the battery for sub-cent prices
  when actual PV may underperform forecast.

### 2026-04-28 (Phase 3d: PV-refill opportunity cost)
- When schedule has cheap (mp<3) PV-surplus daylight ahead, replacement
  cost = avg PV-time market price (forgone feed-in revenue), not the
  pre-sunrise grid-charge effective price. Was blocking evening feed-in
  at 13-16c because minReplacementEffPrice computed against a hypothetical
  24c grid recharge that never happens — tomorrow's PV does the recharge
  for free. New `cheapPvRefillAhead` scans the WHOLE schedule (not just
  [0, horizonIdx)) so post-horizon PV is visible. Falls back to original
  rule on cloudy days. Min ensures we never raise the threshold.

### 2026-04-25 (Retire infra.plan)
- Removed `write_plan`, `query_prev_plan`, `set_topic_prev_plan`.
- Removed prev_plan refs from cronplus + `inject_manual`.
- `join_results.count` 9 -> 8.
- Optimizer: dropped msg2 build; output[1] now unwired
  (`return [msg, null, msg3]`). Forward-looking schedule lives in
  `forecast.batteryplan` only.

### 2026-04-25 (Forecast write: batteryplan)
- Added `to_batteryplan_points` (function) and `write_batteryplan`
  (influxdb batch -> forecast db, measurement `batteryplan`).
- Wired optimizer output[0] to the new chain alongside `debug_schedule`.
- Optimizer output gains numeric `t` (epoch ms) alongside the existing
  human-readable `time` string; new function uses `t` for the influx
  point timestamp (the localized `time` is not Date-parseable).
- Fields per slot: state, predictedSoc, marketPrice, PvPower.
- Same slot timestamp overwrites prior forecasts (forward-looking view).

### 2026-04-25 (Phase 3f: cheapest-ep selection within profit-charge stretch)
- Replaced the time-greedy runtime profit-charge branch (Phase 4) with
  Phase 3f planning. Within each ep<0 stretch, Phase 3f computes K =
  floor((100 - entrySoc) / soc_per_charge), sorts stretch slots by ep ASC,
  and marks the K cheapest-ep slots as _plan='charge'. The existing
  Phase 4 plan==='charge' branch executes them. Higher-ep slots in the
  stretch are left unplanned and fall through to the PV-surplus / curtail
  branches. Without this, the time-first runtime branch would saturate
  the battery on the early (less-negative) slots and curtail the
  later, deeper-negative slots — leaving money on the table. Forward-
  validate no longer relies on isProfitChargeSlot since _plan is now
  authoritative for charging.

### 2026-04-25 (Phase 3e: pre-discharge for profit-charge windows)
- Phase 3e plans pre-discharge feed-in BEFORE consecutive ep<0 stretches
  that would saturate the battery. Without it, paid-to-import slots after
  the SOC cap are curtailed (e.g. mid-day -19ct slots when battery filled
  to 100% by earlier neg-eff slots). Phase 3e walks the schedule, simulates
  SOC including runtime profit-charge, identifies overflow per stretch,
  and assigns 'feedin_capacity' to the highest-mp earlier unplanned slots
  until the overflow is consumed. Round-trip threshold is `mp > minEp +
  cycle_wear` (cycle_wear=3ct), which any positive mp clears for deep
  neg-eff windows. The forward-validate now also handles 'feedin_capacity'
  and models profit-charge so evening feed-ins aren't incorrectly demoted.
  isProfitChargeSlot() helper centralises the runtime-charge predicate
  (used by Phase 3c trajectory, Phase 3d projections, Phase 3e detection,
  forward-validate).

### 2026-04-25 (profit-charge at neg effective price)
- Added profit-charge branch: whenever effectivePrice < 0 and SOC < 95%
  the optimizer now goes state=1 / MAX_CHARGE_W, capturing the paid-to-
  import value regardless of future load needs. Previously grid charging
  required SOC to drop below MIN_SOC+5 future-need threshold, so deeply
  negative-price slots on sunny days produced PV-only charging while
  free-money grid intake was left on the table. Inserted ahead of the
  "Strong PV surplus" branch so PV+grid combine up to the charger limit;
  preemptive-night exclusion preserved (no grid charging at night before
  next-day solar). Feed-in-at-neg-price hard guard untouched — import
  and export sides are independent.

### 2026-04-24 (round-trip guard fix)
- Round-trip feed-in guard now excludes PV-surplus slots from the
  replacement-cost calculation in BOTH Phase 3d and preemptive.
  Previously the cheapest future effectivePrice was dragged down by
  mid-day glut slots (e.g. -9ct), but we can't actually grid-charge
  during glut — PV refills the battery for free. The true replacement
  cost is paid when PV < load (morning dead zone, late night), where
  grid serves load or recharges the battery at effective ~22ct.
  Preemptive filter now also restricts to pre-sunrise + future-only
  (was including past slots because the past-slot trim happens after
  the preemptive block).

### 2026-04-24 (preemptive round-trip guard)
- Preemptive discharge now applies the same round-trip economic check
  that Phase 3d feed-in uses. Each candidate evening slot's marketPrice
  must exceed the cheapest future effectivePrice in slots where grid
  charging is plausibly needed (pvPower < loadEst + 500W). Without the
  check, the optimizer was feeding to grid at ~11ct evening and then
  planning morning grid charges at ~22ct effective — a ~11ct/kWh loss
  per cycle. PV-glut slots are excluded from the replacement set because
  the battery can't actually be charged from them (it's filled by PV
  surplus directly).

### 2026-04-24 (target SOC raised)
- Preemptive sunrise target SOC bumped. Floor is now 30% when
  neg-prices confirm the glut (was 20%), else 40% (was 35%). The
  hard cap in the neg-price branch is now 30% (was 20%). Experience
  showed the earlier 20% target left too little morning reserve on
  mixed-forecast days; the glut day can still absorb plenty of PV
  starting from 30%.

## Changelog

### 2026-04-24 (schedule → global.pp.prediction)
- Battery Optimizer now writes its first-output schedule array
  (`output`) into `global.pp.prediction` (file store) just before
  returning, so downstream flows can consume the planned schedule
  without subscribing to the optimizer's output messages. Uses the
  `file` store to match the existing `pp` object. Reverted the
  earlier attempt that stored the raw pv_history array there.

### 2026-04-24 (later)
- Preemptive sunrise target SOC: floor raised and made conditional on
  neg-price confirmation. Was `max(15, 100-capacityNeededPct)`; now
  `max(socFloor, 100-capacityNeededPct)` where socFloor = 20 when
  negPriceSlots >= 4 (external confirmation of glut) else 35. The old
  15% floor trusted the PV forecast alone and left no buffer for the
  morning peak if PV underperformed.

### 2026-04-24
- Rolling-horizon refill detection now uses relative thresholds: flag
  `dipped` when passive SOC falls ≥5 points below its running peak, set
  horizon at first recovery within 3 points of that peak. Absolute
  70/85 thresholds missed high-starting-SOC runs where the overnight
  trough never broke 70, collapsing the feed-in budget to 0 and
  skipping legitimate evening peaks.
- Replace bare `new Date()` with `new Date(Date.now())` in the pv_history
  day-of-year filter so regression tests' Date.now mock is respected
  (V8's no-arg `new Date()` bypasses mocked Date.now).
- Scenario 1 now passes (8/8).
- Load estimator now uses 7-day history with per-hour MEDIAN across
  days (was: last 24h, single value per hour overwritten by final
  row — a single-day spike like EV charging at 07:00 poisoned every
  future 07:00 forecast). Hours with <3 samples fall back to
  AVG_LOAD_W. Also widened InfluxDB query from 24h to 7d.
- Preemptive glut day is now the current-or-next daylight period,
  so preemptive still runs when the cron fires after sunrise on
  the glut day itself (old code required a night→day transition
  and silently disabled preemptive in that case).
- Preemptive eligibility window extended from "night + pre-PV morning"
  to "now → post-glut sunrise", sorted by price DESC, so the morning
  peak wins over sub-1ct pre-dawn slots.
- Preemptive drain sizing projects SOC with PV+load (+ overflow above
  100%) across the window instead of assuming load-only.
- Feed-in budget projection continues past horizonIdx through the next
  daylight period to catch PV curtailment after the refill slot.
- Added regression scenarios 7 and 8.

### 2026-04-17
- Phase 3d: add round-trip economic check on planned feed-in.
  Non-overflow feed-in now requires marketPrice > cheapest future
  effectivePrice (marketPrice + gridFee) among unplanned slots.
  Overflow (curtailment) SOC remains exempt — pure revenue.
  Fixes: evening feed-in followed by morning grid-charge at a net loss.

name: to batteryplan points
